body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest

Six federal agencies must finalize GENIUS Act stablecoin rules by July 18; stablecoin market cap posted its biggest monthly drop in nearly four years in June even as transaction volume hit a record $1.79T.
Stablecoin News
GENIUS Act rules due July 18
Six federal agencies are finalizing stablecoin rules under the GENIUS Act; payment platforms like Stripe and Block must now charter a stablecoin bank or exit the market.
Circle wins OCC trust bank charter
Circle received an OCC charter on July 10, letting it manage USDC reserves directly; shares surged as competition with Tether intensifies.
Stablecoin market cap posts biggest drop in 4 years
Total stablecoin market cap fell $7.7B in June, led by USDT and USDC declines, even as adjusted transaction volume hit a record $1.79T, up 63% month over month.
Ripple secures full MiCA authorization
Luxembourg's CSSF granted Ripple full MiCA CASP authorization on July 6, enabling regulated stablecoin services across all 30 EEA countries.
Hacking & Security Incidents
Aptos flaw could have exposed $70B

White-hat firm Hexens found a critical Aptos flaw with a near-90% attack success rate that could have hit stablecoins and cross-chain bridges; a validator patch shipped before public disclosure, so no funds were lost.
'Ill Bloom' exploit drains $5M+ from wallets
Attackers exploited a vulnerability dubbed 'Ill Bloom' to drain more than $5 million from cryptocurrency wallets in recent days.
H1 2026 hack losses top $950M
Crypto platforms suffered 207 publicly reported attacks in H1 2026 totaling roughly $955M-$972M in losses, with North Korea-linked hackers responsible for about 66% of stolen funds.
Humanity Protocol breach traced to malware
June's Humanity Protocol hack, which cost over $30M, stemmed from private keys backed up on a malware-infected developer machine.
